hero_subtitle: SFTY organizes audit logs and compliance reviews with precise role-based permissions to protect sensitive evidence and maintain institutional trust for regulated digital asset teams.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Audit access control remains fragmented and unclear
problem_description: Compliance teams and auditors face difficulty tracing user actions and securing sensitive review records across multiple systems. Without clear role-based permissions and consistent audit log governance, maintaining defensible oversight and meeting institutional expectations grows increasingly complex.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY centralizes role-based permissions, user action logs and evidence access into one governed compliance layer so VASP teams can confidently manage audit trails and secure sensitive review records.
workflow_title: From access policies to audit log control
workflow_description: SFTY maps role-based permissions through controlled access, audit logging, evidence retention, and review workflows to support secure compliance governance and institutional readiness.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond manual access. Controlled audit evidence
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides regulated businesses a provider-neutral layer to govern audit log access, preserve evidence integrity, and support clear operational control over compliance review workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports regulated VASPs with controlled role-based access, preserving audit evidence and enabling deployment-ready governance for secure, defensible compliance operations.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support role-based access to audit logs
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how audit evidence access is controlled by roles
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how case review logs preserve traceable decision history
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe secure deployment options for enterprise audit readiness
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY helps VASP compliance teams connect scattered checks into structured workflows with role-based access, audit logs, evidence preservation, case management and reporting workflows support.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY control access to audit logs?
faq_2_answer: SFTY implements role-based permissions to restrict access to sensitive audit logs, decision records and evidence, supporting only authorized users can review or modify compliance data.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY maintains detailed case histories, decision logs and linked evidence packages to support audit readiness and defensible internal and external compliance reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one compliance operating layer without disrupting existing workflows or data sources.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above current providers and orchestrates their outputs into connected compliance processes rather than replacing them.
final_title: Structure audit access with role-based control
final_description: Review how SFTY supports controlled access to audit logs, evidence and sensitive compliance records for VASP compliance teams.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
